fresh_facts.claim: Two US carrier strike groups (USS Gerald R. Ford and USS Abraham Lincoln) are operating in the Middle East region during the 2026 Iran conflict.
The USS Gerald R. Ford's air wings are supported by 14 US Air Force refueling tankers stationed at Israel's Ben Gurion Airport.
US B-1B Lancers have arrived at RAF Fairford for defensive operations in the Middle East.
Vermont Air National Guard's 158th Fighter Wing is deploying as part of Operation Epic Fury.
Pentagon officials confirmed no US ground troops are currently inside Iran as of March 10, 2026.
Joint US-Israel strikes on February 28, 2026 targeted central Tehran with F-22 fighter jets deployed to Ovda Airbase.
US strikes hit over 1,000 targets in the first 24 hours of operations, focusing on Iranian missile infrastructure.
Iran launched ballistic missiles and drones that were intercepted by US-partner air defenses from Qatar, UAE, Kuwait, Jordan, and Saudi Arabia.
Iran struck near the US Navy's Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain.
A fourth US service member death was reported as of March 2, 2026 during Operation Epic Fury.
Iran's parliament speaker rejected ceasefire talks on March 2, 2026.
President Masoud Pezeshkian expressed regret for attacks on neighbors and suspended unprovoked strikes on March 7, 2026.
By March 8, 2026, President Pezeshkian reverted to a harder line, threatening decisive responses to military pressure.
Hardline officials criticized President Pezeshkian's March 7 statements on March 8, 2026, revealing leadership rifts.
An Assembly of Experts member reported a vote on March 8, 2026 for Mojtaba Khamenei as successor with IRGC support.
Saudi Arabia warned Iran on March 8, 2026 against attacks on its territory or energy sites, potentially opening bases to US operations.
Trump stated opposition to Mojtaba Khamenei as successor to Iran's Supreme Leader on March 5, 2026.
Trump stated no deal with Iran except unconditional surrender.
IDF strikes hit IRGC headquarters, missile sites at Parchin, Shahroud, and Khojir, leadership facilities, and detention centers between March 6-8, 2026.
IRGC issued evacuation orders in border areas following Israeli strikes.
Israeli strikes on detention centers caused jailbreaks as of March 8, 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: Intensive US-Israeli military campaign continues with sustained airstrikes targeting Iranian military infrastructure, while two US carrier strike groups, B-1B bombers, and air refueling assets maintain operational pressure. Iran exhibits internal leadership rifts between hardliners and moderates, with Mojtaba Khamenei consolidating succession amid explicit US rejection of his legitimacy and degraded military capabilities.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Israeli Air Force completed coordinated airstrikes on March 9-10 targeting six major Iranian airbases and IRGC facilities across Tehran, East Isfahan, and Shiraz, while US military assets including two carrier strike groups, B-1B Lancers at RAF Fairford, and 14 refueling tankers at Ben Gurion provide sustained operational support.
